title: Web Design Sydney
meta_title: Web Design Sydney | Sites from $750 | OptiTide
meta_description: Web design Sydney businesses can rely on. Australian owned, working remotely on Perth (AWST) hours. Sites from $750 one-off, GST included, no lock-in contracts.
intro: OptiTide builds websites for businesses in Sydney. All work is done remotely, and we work to Perth time — Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST — so feedback you send at the end of your day arrives with hours of our day still to run. Sites start at $750 one-off, GST included, with no lock-in contracts.
body_html:
Working with Sydney businesses, remotely, on Perth hours

OptiTide builds websites for businesses in Sydney, working to Perth time — Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST. There is no Sydney office and we are not going to pretend there is; there is no office anywhere, because all of the work is done remotely. Everything runs that way: briefing, design, revisions and launch. You get a client portal where you can track your project, message us, and view and pay your invoices, so you always know where things are up to without having to chase anyone.

For a lot of businesses that is not a compromise. What actually decides whether a website project goes well is whether the person building it replies to you, explains their decisions in plain English, and hands over something you can live with for the next few years. A shared postcode has never guaranteed any of that.

OptiTide is Michael James Long trading as OptiTide, ABN 38 163 865 712, Australian owned and operated, working with clients across Australia.

Does working remotely affect your local search results?

No. Local rankings in Sydney are driven by your business details: your Google Business Profile, your address, your service area, your reviews and the content on your own site. All of that describes you, not your web designer. The site is built to reflect where you actually trade, and if local visibility is the priority, SEO is a separate monthly service that starts at $750 per month.

The time difference works in your favour

Sydney runs two hours ahead of AWST in winter and three hours ahead once daylight saving starts. We work Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST — Perth time — kept deliberately late into the evening.

The arithmetic is the point. You look at your site at the end of your day and send through a list of changes at 5 PM Sydney time. That lands at 2 PM or 3 PM on our clock, with several hours of our working day still to run, and those hours fall while your office is closed. Someone working your own hours is shutting the door at the moment you hit send, so the same email reaches the start of their next day rather than the middle of ours.

That is the whole of the advantage, and it is worth being precise about it: more of our working day sits after the end of yours, which is why a client on the east coast can send something in the evening and look at the result the next morning. How long any particular change takes still depends on what the change is. Ask when you send it and we will tell you.

What we build

Our web design work covers new sites and rebuilds of sites that have stopped pulling their weight. Exactly what goes into a build depends on the tier you choose, and that detail lives on the web design page. The thinking behind the work does not change between tiers:

  • Phone first. People reach small business websites on a phone, so the layout is designed for a narrow screen and adapted upward, rather than the other way around.
  • Speed. A page that takes its time loses people before they read a word. Images are compressed properly and the build is kept lean.
  • A clear path to enquiry. Every page should make it obvious what to do next, whether that is a quote form, a booking or an email.
  • Search foundations. Sensible page titles, headings and clean URLs, so the site can be read properly from the day it goes live.
  • Plain English. You should be able to understand what was built and why, without a glossary and without having to take anyone's word for it.

What web design in Sydney costs

Prices are published rather than invented on a call. They include GST, they are in Australian dollars, and there are no lock-in contracts.

  • Starter: $750 one-off. Suits a business that needs a straightforward, credible site and does not need much beyond that.
  • Business: $1,500 one-off. Suits a business with more to explain, more services to cover, or more room to grow into.
  • Custom: quoted. For larger builds, online stores and anything with unusual requirements. We scope the job and quote it before work starts.

What each tier covers is set out on the web design page, and the full range sits on the services page. If you are not sure which one fits, describe the job and we will tell you.

How a project runs

You tell us what you need and we quote. Once you accept, you fill in a project brief covering your business, your customers, the pages you want and any brand assets you already have. We design, you review, you send changes, and when you are happy the site goes live.

The brief, the files, the messages and the invoices all sit in your client portal rather than being scattered through an email thread. That matters more than it sounds when you are two or three hours ahead and asleep for part of our working day, because you can open the portal at any hour and see the current state of things without waiting for someone to answer. There is more on how we work.

What to ask any web designer before you hire them

Worth asking whoever you approach, including us.

  • What is the total price, and what makes it go up? A number you can see before you make contact is a good sign. Ours are above.
  • Am I locked into anything? Ask about minimum terms and exit conditions on anything monthly. OptiTide has no lock-in contracts.
  • What are your hours? If you are on the east coast, ask what time their day ends in your timezone. Ours run to 9 PM AWST, Monday to Friday.
  • How do I pay? OptiTide takes PayID and bank transfer, Skrill, PayPal and Payoneer, and every invoice appears in your portal.
  • Where do I see what is happening? Ask how you track progress and raise questions once the job has started.

After the site is live

A launch is a starting point rather than a finish line, and the ongoing services are separate, monthly and optional. Hosting is $25 per month unmanaged or $50 per month managed. SEO is $750, $1,500 or $2,500 per month depending on the tier. Social media is $250 per month. Nothing is bundled in without you asking for it, and nothing is locked into a contract.

Getting started

Tell us what your business does, what the current site does badly, and roughly when you want the new one live. We will tell you which tier fits and what it costs, in writing, before anything starts. Get in touch through the contact page, or read more about OptiTide first.

faqs.q: Do you have an office in Sydney?
faqs.a: No, and there is no office in Perth either — all of the work is done remotely. What we do keep is Perth hours, Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST, so a Sydney client is dealing with someone whose day runs past the end of theirs. Briefing, design, revisions and messages all run through email and your client portal, so nothing in the process depends on meeting in person. We would rather say that plainly than list a virtual address.

faqs.q: How much does a website cost?
faqs.a: The Starter tier is $750 as a one-off and the Business tier is $1,500 as a one-off, both in Australian dollars and both GST inclusive. Larger builds, online stores and anything with unusual requirements are quoted after we scope the work, and you get the price before anything begins. There are no lock-in contracts.

faqs.q: How does the time difference actually work day to day?
faqs.a: Sydney is two hours ahead of AWST, or three during daylight saving. We work Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST, so an email you send at 5 PM Sydney time reaches us at 2 PM or 3 PM our time, with several hours of our day still to run — hours that fall while your office is closed. That is why a Sydney client can send something in the evening and look at the result the next morning. How long a specific change takes depends on what it is, so ask when you send it.

faqs.q: How do I pay, and where do I find my invoices?
faqs.a: We accept PayID and bank transfer, Skrill, PayPal and Payoneer. Every invoice appears in your client portal, where you can view it and pay it alongside the rest of your project. All prices we quote include GST.

faqs.q: Do I have to buy hosting and SEO as well?
faqs.a: No. The build is a one-off price and stands on its own; the ongoing services are separate and optional. If you want us to handle hosting it is $25 per month unmanaged or $50 per month managed. SEO is $750, $1,500 or $2,500 per month depending on the tier, and social media is $250 per month. None of them is a lock-in contract.
